question: Assume you have an interface called Payable as follows: public interface Payable{ double getPaymentAmount(); } If you have another class Invoice that implements this interface and getQuantity() and getPricePerItem() methods that returns quantity and price per item, which of the following is the correct implementation?
options: public abstract class Invoice implements Payable{ public double getPaymentAmount () { return getQuantity() * getPricePerItem(); } }
public class Invoice implements Payable{ public double getPaymentAmount () { return getQuantity() * getPricePerItem(); } }
public class Invoice implements Payable{ public getPaymentAmount () { return getQuantity() * getPricePerItem(); } }
public class Invoice extends Payable{ public double getPaymentAmount() { return getQuantity() * getPricePerItem(); } }
